changes_applied.fix: Rewrite description with Playwright pairing + performance triggers
changes_applied.details: New description clarifies when to pick chrome-devtools vs web-validation (performance/profiling vs validation flow) and adds performance-specific triggers: 'Core Web Vitals', 'Lighthouse audit', 'memory snapshot', 'LCP/CLS/FID'. 4 new YAML triggers.

changes_skipped.reason: Skill is well-organized (222 lines) with clear MCP tool references. Description was the gap.

description_before: Deep browser inspection via Chrome DevTools MCP: performance profiling (Core Web Vitals), Lighthouse audits, network inspection, console monitoring, memory snapshots. Use for debugging, perf, a11y.
description_after: Use whenever you need deep browser inspection that Playwright alone can't provide — live DevTools features, real Chrome behavior, Core Web Vitals, Lighthouse audits, network waterfall inspection, console monitoring across sessions, memory/heap snapshots. Pairs with web-validation (which handles the validation flow) by providing the performance/debugging/profiling side. Reach for it on phrases like 'check performance', 'Lighthouse score', 'Core Web Vitals', 'why is this slow', 'inspect the network tab', 'memory leak', 'LCP/CLS/FID', or when browser-specific debugging is needed beyond screenshots.
self_review_notes: Positioning against web-validation matters — both use browser automation, but this skill is specifically for when Playwright-level validation isn't enough and you need DevTools-specific features.
